title: Neuro-respiratory pathology in spinocerebellar ataxia.
abstract: The spinocerebellar ataxias (SCA) are a heterogeneous group of neurodegenerative disorders with an autosomal dominant inheritance. Symptoms include poor coordination and balance, peripheral neuropathy, impaired vision, incontinence, respiratory insufficiency, dysphagia, and dysarthria. Although many patients with SCA have respiratory-related complications, the exact mechanism and extent of this pathology remain unclear. This review aims to provide an update on the recent clinical and preclinical scientific findings on neuropathology causing respiratory insufficiency in SCA.
